---
title: "Explorable lattice — interactive 3D viewer"
description: >-
  A live, rotatable 3D model of an A=1 lattice state, produced by the
  dcl-lattice-viewer tool. Parity colouring shows the bipartite RGB/CMY
  sublattice split that defines the substrate.
categories: [3D model, visualization, dcl-lattice-viewer]
resources:
  - explorable-lattice.glb
format:
  html:
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <script type="module" src="https://unpkg.com/@google/model-viewer@^4.0.0/dist/model-viewer.min.js"></script>
---

A **live, explorable** A=1 lattice state — drag to rotate, scroll to zoom,
right-drag to pan, double-click to reset. It is rendered straight from
lattice field data by [`dcl-lattice-viewer`](https://github.com/JackDMenendez/dcl-lattice-viewer)
(v0.1.0), the downstream visualization tool for the `dcl-core` engine.

```{=html}
<model-viewer
  src="explorable-lattice.glb"
  alt="A roughly spherical cloud of small cubes on a 21x21x21 lattice, warm-coloured cubes on one sublattice interleaved with cool-coloured cubes on the other, densest at the centre and fading toward the edges."
  shadow-intensity="1"
  camera-controls
  auto-rotate
  touch-action="pan-y"
  style="width: 100%; height: 520px; background-color: #111111;">
  <div slot="poster" style="display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; height:100%;">
    <p style="text-align:center; padding:1rem; color:#ccc;">
      Loading the lattice model… if nothing appears, your browser may not
      support WebGL.
    </p>
  </div>
</model-viewer>
```

## What you are looking at

This is a **demonstration lattice state** — a minimum-uncertainty Gaussian
wavepacket synthesised by the viewer's `demo` command, not an experiment
run. Even so, it is drawn by exactly the rules the tool applies to real
`dcl-core` dumps:

- **Position** — each cube sits at its lattice coordinate, recentred on
  the lattice ($21 \times 21 \times 21$ here).
- **Size** — $\sqrt{}$-scaled by the total token count at the site, so the
  dense core of the wavepacket reads larger than the tail.
- **Colour** — this model uses **parity** mode: the RGB (even) sublattice
  is warm, the CMY (odd) sublattice is cool. The interleaved warm/cool
  cubes *are* the bipartite structure on which the whole framework rests —
  two interpenetrating sublattices related by the tick rule. (The tool also
  offers `phase`, a full colour wheel keyed to the token-weighted phase,
  and `count`, a dark-to-amber ramp.)

## Make your own

The viewer turns the `.npy` / `.npz` token-and-phase fields that `dcl-core`
experiments write into a `.glb` (for a `<model-viewer>` like the one above)
and a self-contained interactive three.js `.html` (orbit, zoom, pan, and —
for a time series — scrub through ticks).

```sh
# install (base = HTML only; the [glb] extra adds .glb export)
pip install "dcl_lattice_viewer[glb] @ git+https://github.com/JackDMenendez/dcl-lattice-viewer@v0.1.0"

# see output immediately, no engine run required
dcl-lattice-viewer demo -o lattice --color parity        # writes lattice.glb + lattice.html

# render a real experiment dump (a directory of .npy, or an .npz)
dcl-lattice-viewer build data/exp_12_run/ -o figures/exp_12 --format both
```

A `4D` array `(T, nx, ny, nz)` is read as a tick sequence and **animates**
in the HTML viewer. The base install needs only NumPy; `.glb` export adds
`trimesh`, and an `[engine]` extra can build frames straight from a live
`dcl-core` session.

## Provenance

- **Tool:** [`dcl-lattice-viewer`](https://github.com/JackDMenendez/dcl-lattice-viewer)
  v0.1.0 (MIT). The model above was produced with
  `dcl-lattice-viewer demo -o explorable-lattice --color parity --shape 21 21 21 --units 40000`.
- **Engine of record:** [`dcl-core`](../papers/index.qmd#dcl-core---python-engine),
  whose `.npy` field format the tool consumes.
- **Substrate:** the bipartite octahedral lattice of
  [Paper I — *Geometry First*](../papers/index.qmd#paper-i---geometry-first);
  see also the [unit-cell model](lattice-unit-cell.qmd) for the substrate's
  geometric primitive.

## Accessibility

The model carries a text description in its `alt` attribute, and a loading
message stands in until WebGL initialises. For a high-resolution still or a
guided walk-through, see the [contact page](../contact.qmd).
